---
title: "drisk"
---

`drisk` is a Python package designed for quick Monte Carlo modelling, with an emphasis
on distribution elicitation and composable models.

The intention is to make Monte Carlo models ergonomic enough for "back of the envelope"
problems in quick scripts or notebooks, while also supporting more advanced methods for
heavier simulation workloads.

`drisk` is in development, and currently supports univariate continuous and discrete
distribution sampling, correlated sampling using Copulas, composable Monte Carlo
models, and decision support workflows such as decision-tree rollback analysis.

This documentation site collects runnable examples and report-style walkthroughs.

## Quick start

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

import drisk as dr
```

Create a positive, right-skewed LogNormal distribution and plot it.

```{python}
volume = dr.LogNormal.elicit(
    lower=800,
    upper=1_500,
    confidence=0.8,
    name="Monthly sales volume",
)

volume.plot(color="steelblue")
```

Create a PERT distribution from a minimum, most-likely, and maximum estimate.

```{python}
margin = dr.PERT.elicit(
    min=20,
    mode=35,
    max=60,
    name="Unit margin",
)

margin.plot(color="darkorange")
```

Compose the distributions with ordinary Python arithmetic, then simulate the result.

```{python}
monthly_profit = volume * margin
samples = monthly_profit.sample(size=50_000, seed=42)
samples[:5]
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4.5))
monthly_profit.plot(ax=ax, color="seagreen")
ax.set_title("Simulated monthly profit")
ax.set_xlabel("Monthly profit")
fig.tight_layout()
```

## Examples

- [Working with Distributions](examples/distributions.qmd): the shared distribution API
for eliciting, fitting, plotting, and sampling.
- [Composing Monte Carlo Models with Distribution
Arithmetic](examples/mc-composition.qmd): a new product launch profit model built from
elicited distributions and ordinary Python arithmetic.
- [Combining Distributions with Mixtures](examples/mixtures.qmd): weighted blends of
univariate distributions for processes with distinct regimes.
- [Correlated Sampling with Copulas](examples/correlated-sampling.qmd): the same product
launch model with correlated input sampling.
- [Decision Trees for Business Decisions](examples/decision-trees.qmd): rollback
analysis for sequential business choices with uncertain outcomes.
- [Composing Discrete and Continuous Distributions](examples/equipment-failures.qmd):
an equipment-failure planning model combining a Poisson event count with continuous
average repair cost and downtime distributions.
